word.definition: Indomitable is a formal word used to describe something that is impossible to defeat or discourage.

sports_fact.text: On June 19, 1846, the first organized baseball game under modern rules was played at Elysian Fields in Hoboken, New Jersey. The Knickerbockers — who wrote the rules — lost 23-1.

market.headline: Why Apple’s War Chest Can’t Win the Memory War
market.summary: The iPhone maker’s clout as a buyer has taken a major hit from the AI boom.

quote.text: Juneteenth has never been a celebration of victory, or an acceptance of the way things are. It's a celebration of progress. It's an affirmation that despite the most painful parts of our history, change is possible –– and there is still so much work to do.
quote.author: Barack Obama

history.text: The village of Knockcroghery, Ireland, is burned by British forces.
